TranslationContext: This file contains Unit Names for the Dwarf Hold Faction.

Translations.Comment: Plural form referring to dwarf fortresses collectively
Standard infantry unit; formal adjective for dwarf-related
Worker unit that excavates; maintains connection to dwarf heritage
Ranged infantry with crossbows; military term for crossbow wielders
Firearms infantry; period-appropriate term for musketeers
Scout/ranger unit; more grounded than transliterating English term
Heavy crossbow weapon; combines bolt + thrower into single term
Siege weapon; using established term for stone-throwing artillery
Elite melee warriors; term retained for recognizable mythological context
Elite guard unit from dwarf mines; 'deep' refers to underground fortresses
Special fire-based artillery; 'infernal' conveys magical/devastating nature
Distinctive dwarf-tech unit; maintains fantastical tone
Leader unit; 'master' implies authority and craftsmanship
Commander of scout/ranger units; clear rank designation
Technical specialist unit; reflects dwarf civilization's engineering prowess
